title: Air-fryer tofu
subtitle: Crispy cubed tofu, no frying, no soggy centre.
excerpt: Firm tofu pressed dry, cubed, tossed in soy sauce and sesame oil, and cooked in the air fryer at 200°C until the outside crisps and the inside stays custardy. Ready in 25 minutes including pressing time. Serves two.
sourceNotes: Air-fryer tofu technique. The pressing-and-coating approach is standard in contemporary plant-based cooking; the soy and sesame marinade draws on Japanese teriyaki proportions.

content.text: The problem with tofu is moisture. Firm tofu is about 80 per cent water by weight. Put it in a hot pan and it steams itself; put it in the air fryer without pressing it first and the same thing happens. Press the water out, coat the surface in oil and seasoning, and the air fryer does what it does best: very hot, very dry heat that crisps the outside fast while the inside stays soft.

Drain {{tofu-firm}} of firm tofu and slice into 2 cm cubes. Spread on several layers of kitchen paper and press another layer on top. Leave for 10 minutes, replacing the paper once if it becomes saturated. The tofu should feel noticeably drier.

Put the cubes in a bowl. Toss with {{soy-sauce-light}} of soy sauce, {{sesame-oil}} of sesame oil and {{rapeseed-oil}} of rapeseed oil. If using cornflour, sprinkle over and toss again to coat each piece in a fine dusty layer.

Preheat the air fryer to 200°C. Arrange the tofu in a single layer in the basket. Cook for 18 to 20 minutes, shaking the basket once halfway through, until the surfaces are golden and firm to the touch.

items.fix: Press for at least 10 minutes; 30 minutes gives better results. Single layer in the basket is non-negotiable.
items.cause: Not pressed long enough, or basket was crowded.
items.symptom: Tofu is soft and sticky, not crispy

items.fix: Preheat the basket and make sure each cube is well coated in oil. Most air-fryer baskets are non-stick but benefit from a light oil coating.
items.cause: Not enough oil, or basket not preheated.

items.fix: Use only extra-firm or firm tofu. Silken tofu won't hold its shape in an air fryer.
items.cause: Silken or soft tofu used instead of firm.

content.text: Air-fryer tofu fits into grain bowls, noodle dishes, stir-fries and salads as a protein. It keeps its crunch for about 20 minutes after cooking; after that it softens as it cools. Add it last, on top, so the texture stays visible. It pairs particularly well with a sesame-ginger dressing, pickled cucumber, and a bowl of jasmine rice.
